inline_comments.message: need to save a handle to this anywhere so it can be shut down?
sonar: iterate over entrySet, thus eliminating need for "get()" on line 151
use a StringBuilder instead of String, and append() instead of "+=" (below)
combine containsKey() and get() call - just use get() and check for non-null
combine this with line 105 or even delete it and combine lines 105 & 106

all of these sleeps add up - is there no way to do this with a CountDownLatch instead?

same comment in other files

should probably do this in an @After method, so it's reset whether or not the test succeeds

same comment below (and in other files)

if these are just Lists, then order is not guaranteed and equals() may fail - may need to convert to Sets first

same comment in other files
maybe use a StandardCoder instead of an ObjectMapper

perhaps use DummyTaskExecutor.getName() so that the class name/package can be changed without breaking this

same comment below
apexEngineService is not needed while terminating. engineServiceHandler is what required for this, and it is available as an instance variable

That would be tricky I think. I might have to check the logs or fire an event(depending on the policy) in the countdownlatch to know if the policy is executed. Anyway, this sleep is not necessarily needed. So removing it.
could just pass a File to decode() and avoid this read altogether
could just pass a File to decode() and avoid this read altogether
could just pass a File to decode() and avoid this read altogether
Is it possible to generalize this and have the case of one model just being the same as calling the multiple case with an array with one entry?
I know it wasn't in there before but we probably should check if the engine is already initialized and throw an exception if it is.
Same here, check for already initialized case.

I don't think this should be in the model loop because it will set up the marshaller/unmarshaller and pairings multiple times, one for each model.

I think we have to combine the inputs and outputs and then call these once, otherwise we'll only have the last set of inputs/outputs defined.

I think you can replace this method by using the utility class:
org.onap.policy.apex.model.policymodel.handling.PolicyModelMerger
Same comment, why not just have a map with one entry for the single model case.

I'm not keen on magic flags.

Maybe treating the single case as not a special case will remove the need for this.

Here each input/output from each model was iterated and the marshaller/unmarshaller was getting added to a map. So, it wouldn't get overwritten. But setting up marshaller/unmarshaller before actually updating the model to the engine can mess up some test cases. So moving this after the model update part.
Done. Thanks, I didn't notice this class earlier.
sonar: probably exceeds cyclomatic complexity - maybe refactor/extract methods out of this

looks like getSchemasMap() can be done once and cached

similar comment below
looks like a duplicate of the code for the left model - maybe refactor/extract a common method
can use assertj for this, too - I THINK there's a hasMessageStartingWith method (or some such)
I assume you know what you're doing ;-)
maybe swap the bodies of the "if" and "else" - then can get rid of the negation (and change "&&" to "||", of course).  That might make the conditional more understandable.
could probably use a stream for this
could probably use stream.reduce() (or some such) for this
should probably change "is having" to "has" here and in the class that generates this message
Yes, the exclusion is added to avoid dependency conflict.

these "for" loops all look the same - perhaps extract a common function (may need to be generic, if the values are of different type).
Another option would be to manipulate maps instead of key sets, then could just use map.addAll(copyMap) for each of these.
these "for" loops all look the same - perhaps extract a common function
looks like a duplicate of the previous method
just a suggestion: could pass "left" or "right" instead of boolean
not sure if sonar will complain or not - might need to add a comment inside the method body or invoke super() - can do that in another review if it's an issue

still kind of messy, but at least it's slightly clearer what it's doing.  If AxPolicyModel had a copy of the identifer, then could use the Model instead of the whole Map.Entry, which would make it a little cleaner still - oh well.
